feature: Scheduled Tasks — edit a cron job (desktop inline form)
user_story: As a subscriber on desktop, I want to edit an existing scheduled task inline so that I can refine its schedule or prompt.
expected_behaviour: Clicking "Edit" on a job row sets `editingName` and renders an inline form with TimezoneSelector, ScheduleBuilder, Delivery select, SessionModeSelector, PromptEditor, and CapabilityChips. The form is pre-populated from `toEditForm(job)` (stripping the date prefix from the message). Submitting calls `PATCH /api/v1/cron/jobs/<name>/` via `useUpdateCronJobMutation`. On success `✓ Saved` flashes green for 2 s. On error the button turns red for 4 s.
inventory_notes: Only one job can be in edit mode at a time (single `editingName` state). Opening a new edit does NOT close the previous one unless the user explicitly cancels.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — edit a cron job (mobile full-screen overlay)
user_story: As a subscriber on mobile, I want a full-screen edit experience for a scheduled task so that the form is usable on a small screen.
expected_behaviour: When `editingName` is set and `isMobile === true` (matched via `window.matchMedia('(max-width: 767px)')`), the edit form is rendered via `createPortal` to `document.body` as a fixed full-screen overlay. The app header is hidden (`visibility: hidden`) and body scroll is locked (`overflow: hidden`) while the overlay is open. A sticky header bar shows the job name, Save, and Cancel buttons. The form content is scrollable. Save calls the same update mutation.
inventory_notes: The overlay is only rendered when `isMobile === true` (starts `null` on SSR, set after mount via matchMedia). Mismatched hydration is possible on first render.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — single-job delete with inline confirm
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a specific scheduled task with a quick confirm so that I don't accidentally remove it.
expected_behaviour: Each job row has a "Delete" button. Clicking it sets that job into "confirming" state: the row background shifts to rose, and inline "Delete? ✓ ✕" buttons replace the Delete button. A 3 s auto-cancel timer resets confirmation if not acted upon. Clicking ✓ calls `DELETE /api/v1/cron/jobs/<name>/` via `useDeleteCronJobMutation`. The job is also deselected from the multi-select set. Clicking ✕ or waiting 3 s cancels. On delete error, the error shows inline with a 4 s auto-clear.
inventory_notes: Delete confirmation is per-row, not a modal, so multiple rows can be in confirming state simultaneously.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — multi-select and bulk delete
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to select and delete multiple scheduled tasks at once so that I can clean up efficiently.
expected_behaviour: Each job row has a checkbox (44px touch target). A "Select all" header row appears when ≥2 jobs exist. Checking rows adds them to `selectedIds`. When any are selected a bulk action bar slides in with: count, "Set foreground", "Set background", "Delete selected", and "Clear" buttons. Clicking "Delete selected" shows a confirming sub-state with "Delete N tasks? This cannot be undone." Confirming calls `POST /api/v1/cron/jobs/bulk-delete/` via `useBulkDeleteCronJobsMutation`. On success shows a toast "N tasks deleted" and clears selection. On error shows an inline error in the bar.
inventory_notes: The bulk action bar uses `max-h` CSS transition (0 → 8rem) for animated reveal/hide.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set multiple tasks to foreground or background mode at once so that I can control which tasks send messages to me.
expected_behaviour: In the bulk action bar, "Set foreground" and "Set background" buttons call `PATCH /api/v1/cron/jobs/bulk-foreground/` via `useBulkUpdateForegroundMutation` with `{ ids, foreground: true/false }`. On success clears selection and shows a toast "N tasks set to foreground/background". On error shows a toast with the error message.

user_story: As a subscriber who visits the old /settings/automations URL, I want to be redirected to the current page so that bookmarks still work.
expected_behaviour: The `automations/page.tsx` is a client component that on mount calls `router.replace('/settings/cron-jobs')` and renders nothing. This is a permanent redirect implemented in the frontend, not via HTTP.
inventory_notes: Because it uses `router.replace`, the `/settings/automations` URL is removed from browser history.

feature: People — entity registry list with curated filter
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see the people my assistant has detected so that I can verify and correct name bindings.
expected_behaviour: Calls `GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/`. Loading shows "Loading…" text with `role=status`. Error shows a rose alert. Empty state shows "No people are tracked yet." When entries exist, shows count ("Showing N of M") and a "Show only curated" checkbox (default ON). Curated = entries with a non-empty relationship or notes field. Non-curated auto-detections are hidden by default (with a count notice). A search input filters by placeholder/name/relationship/notes across visible entries. When the filter yields nothing a helpful message appears with buttons to clear filters.
inventory_notes: The page loads `fetchPIIDenylist` concurrently for the denylist section.

feature: People — edit entity name, relationship, and notes
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit the name, relationship, and notes for a detected person so that my assistant uses accurate information.
expected_behaviour: Each entry card shows three text inputs (Name, Relationship, Notes) pre-filled from the draft state. Edits are tracked in a local `drafts` map (keyed by placeholder). The Save button is enabled only when `dirty` (draft !== server state) and is disabled when another mutation is pending for that row. Clicking Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/` with the draft as the patch body. On success the entity-registry query is invalidated. On error, an inline rose alert appears per-row. Clicking Cancel resets the draft to the server value.
inventory_notes: Draft state persists across query refetches (only new entries without a draft are seeded from server data). Deleted entries are pruned from drafts.

feature: People — delete an entity registry entry
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a name binding so that my assistant stops using that placeholder mapping.
expected_behaviour: Each entry card has a "Delete" button (rose-styled). Clicking calls `window.confirm()` with a message explaining the scope. If confirmed, calls `DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/`. On success the query is invalidated and the row disappears. On error, the per-row error map is set showing the error message inline.
inventory_notes: This is the one place in Settings that uses `window.confirm()` for confirmation instead of an inline or modal pattern.

feature: People — ignore a name (add to PII denylist)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a detected name as not-personal so that my assistant stops redacting it in future messages.
expected_behaviour: Each entry row has an "Ignore" button, disabled if the entry has no name. Clicking calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/` with `{ name }`. On success the denylist query is invalidated and the row shows "Ignored — won't be redacted" instead of the Ignore button (checked via `denylistKeys.has(name.toLowerCase())`). Button is disabled while saving, deleting, or already ignored.

feature: People — bulk select and ignore multiple entries
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to bulk-ignore multiple detected names so that I can quickly clean up false-positive auto-detections.
expected_behaviour: Each entry row has a checkbox. A "Select all visible" checkbox with indeterminate state handles visible-filtered rows. When ≥1 is selected, a sticky bulk action bar appears with "Ignore N selected" button (rose, glow). Clicking calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/bulk/` with `{ names: [...] }`. On success clears selection and invalidates denylist query. If some entries were skipped (empty/invalid names) shows a partial-success message. On hard error shows a rose error.
inventory_notes: Select-all operates only over currently-visible filtered entries, not all entries in the registry.

feature: People — ignored words list and re-enable redaction
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which words I've marked as not-personal and restore redaction for any of them so that I can correct mistakes.
expected_behaviour: A separate SectionCard below the entity registry calls `GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/`. Loading shows "Loading…" with role=status. Error shows a rose alert. Empty state says "Nothing ignored yet." Each entry shows the word key and the `decided_at` date. A "Re-enable redaction" button calls `DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/<key>/`. On error falls back to `window.alert()` (low-effort path per comment). Button shows "Re-enabling…" while pending.
inventory_notes: Uses `window.alert()` for the error case in undo-ignore, unlike the standard inline error pattern used everywhere else on the page.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose which AI model my assistant uses so that I can balance capability and cost.
expected_behaviour: Calls `GET /api/v1/tenants/me/` and `GET /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/`. Loading shows `<SectionCardSkeleton lines={5}>`. Models are rendered as a responsive grid of button cards. Each card shows: name, `<IntelligenceMeter>`, tagline, input/output rate. State badges: "Active" (accent, model is applied), "Switching…" (amber pulse, selected but not yet applied), "Coming Soon" (disabled), "Locked" (BYO required but not connected), "Free" (accent outline). Clicking a non-coming-soon available model calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/preferred-model/` with `{ preferred_model }`. While any mutation is pending all cards get `opacity-60`. Clicking a BYO-gated model that is locked opens the Connect Anthropic modal instead.
inventory_notes: The active model is `tenant.effective_model || tenant.preferred_model || DEFAULT_MODEL`. The applied model is `tenant.applied_model || activeModel`. Divergence between these two triggers "Switching…". Limited-time offer cards are hidden when `offer.active` is false.

feature: AI Provider — limited-time free model offer banner
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to be informed about a free model offer so that I can take advantage of it without being charged tokens.
expected_behaviour: When `tenant.free_model_offer.active` is true, a bordered accent panel appears above the model grid explaining the offer model name and fallback model. The offer model card also has a "Free" badge. The offer model card only appears in `visibleModels` when `offerActive` is true (filtered out otherwise).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose a lighter model for background tasks so that I stretch my monthly budget further.
expected_behaviour: The "Scheduled Task Models" section lists 5 tasks (Morning Briefing, Evening Check-in, Week Ahead Review, Background Tasks, Heartbeat Check-in). Each has a `<select>` with "Use default (current model)" plus all available active models. Changing any select immediately calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/task-model-preferences/` with `{ task_model_preferences: { [task.slug]: selectedModelId } }` via `useTaskModelPreferencesMutation`. All selects are disabled while the mutation is pending. BYO-locked and limited-time-offer models (when offer inactive) are filtered from the per-task options.
inventory_notes: Changes are described as applying "within ~30 seconds".

feature: AI Provider — BYO Anthropic cards (when byo_models_enabled)
user_story: As a subscriber with BYO enabled on my tier, I want to see Anthropic and OpenAI provider cards so that I know I can bring my own credentials.
expected_behaviour: When `tenant.byo_models_enabled` is true, a "Use your own subscription" SectionCard appears above the model grid, containing `<BYOProviderCard>` for Anthropic and OpenAI. The Anthropic card shows: status pill (Not connected / pending / verified / error/expired), body text, eyebrow, and a CTA button. CTA label: "Connect →" (no cred), "Cancel" (pending), "Disconnect" (verified), "Reconnect →" (expired/error). OpenAI card is permanently `disabled` with a "Coming soon" badge. If the cred has `last_error` and is in error state, the error is shown in a rose box.
inventory_notes: The BYO section is completely hidden when byo_models_enabled is false.

feature: AI Provider — Connect Anthropic modal (2-step flow)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Anthropic Claude account so that inference is billed to my own subscription.
expected_behaviour: Step 1 (info): Shows `claude setup-token` CLI command with a Copy button (clipboard API, 2.5 s "Copied" state). Shows privacy disclosure (encrypted, isolated container, revocable). Clicking "I have my token →" moves to Step 2. Step 2 (paste): A textarea (min 32 chars required, spellCheck off) for the OAuth token. "Connect" button is disabled until token ≥32 chars. On submit calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/` with `{ provider:'anthropic', mode:'cli_subscription', token }`. On success modal closes and `onSettled` invalidates byo-credentials query. On error: 400 → format error, 502 → storage failure, other → generic. Modal always closeable via Escape, backdrop click, or ✕ button; closing mid-request aborts the fetch via `AbortController`. State resets 200 ms after close.
inventory_notes: The modal prevents the user from being trapped: Cancel always works even while the request is in flight (AbortController abort). Pending cred has no automatic verifier — the CTA on BYOProviderCard shows "Cancel" in pending state.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to disconnect my Anthropic subscription so that my assistant reverts to the platform's default model.
expected_behaviour: Modal opens with `open={true}` when `openModal === 'disconnect-anthropic'`. Shows: warning icon, fallback model name (computed from `DEFAULT_MODEL`), bullet list of consequences (credential deleted from Key Vault, container restarts ~30s, Claude Sonnet disappears from picker). "Disconnect" button calls `DELETE /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/<id>/`. On success modal closes; `onSettled` invalidates credentials. On error, rose alert appears in the modal. Cancel always available and aborts in-flight request. State resets 200 ms after close.
